delegationRole: The Pakistani delegation, pressing the unimplemented 1948 UN plebiscite, internationalizing the dispute against Indian efforts to domesticate it, and refusing any outcome short of eventual accession.
fundamentalPrinciples: Jammu and Kashmir is an inalienable part of Pakistani territory.
Resist Indian hegemony in the region.
Protecting Kashmiri Muslims and resisting the Indian occupation is an integral part of our national identity.

desiredEndStates.primary: A plebiscite on the original 1948 terms — accession to India or Pakistan, with no independence option — held after Indian troop withdrawal, delivering Jammu and Kashmir to Pakistan.
desiredEndStates.alternate: Failing that, prevention: no independence, no expansion of Indian authority, no recognition of the Line of Control, and an internationalized human rights file that keeps the dispute alive.

keyPositionsByIssue.TERRITORY: We will not under any circumstances allow Azad Kashmir to become part of an independent or autonomous Kashmir, and we refuse to recognize the Line of Control as an official border with India.
keyPositionsByIssue.LOC-STATUS: Recognition of the LoC as an international boundary is to be prevented at all costs; it would end the dispute on Indian terms.
keyPositionsByIssue.PLEBISCITE: The 1948 UN-mandated plebiscite, with accession as the only options, is the sole viable route. It need not include Aksai Chin. Preconditions are Indian troop withdrawal and an end to violent repression.
keyPositionsByIssue.GOVERNANCE: Oppose Article 370 as a solution: it still lets India legislate on defense, keep troops, and interfere in Kashmiri governance. Support further autonomy only under Pakistani sovereignty. Promote demilitarization and joint India–Pakistan governance until the referendum is held.
keyPositionsByIssue.KASHMIRI-DELEGATION: They must renounce any present claim to represent Azad Kashmir. Until then we work to delegitimize their claim to speak for all Kashmiris; we support their positions only where those weaken India.
keyPositionsByIssue.HUMAN-RIGHTS: Indian forces have used torture, mass killings, forced disappearances and extrajudicial killing; AFSPA permits detention for two years without charge. This forum is the opportunity to expose India's contradiction as a secular state.
keyPositionsByIssue.INVESTIGATIONS: Support a UN investigation limited to actions taken by Indian security forces. Oppose any investigation extending to militant groups, which would let investigators focus on Lashkar-e-Taiba or Jaish-e-Muhammad and attempt to blame Pakistan.
keyPositionsByIssue.IDP-REFUGEES: The displaced populations in Azad Kashmir and Pakistan are unsupportable on our resources; we need international aid, so long as that aid requires no concessions on our Kashmir policy.
keyPositionsByIssue.TRIFURCATION: Oppose the Indian decision to split occupied Jammu and Kashmir into multiple units — it splits the Muslim population and makes future accession harder.

redLines: No agreement in which all of Jammu and Kashmir is not a part of Pakistan.
No recognition of the Line of Control as an international border.
No investigation whose mandate extends to militant groups operating from Pakistani territory.
No international assistance conditioned on concessions to our Kashmir policy.
batna: Keep the dispute internationalized and unresolved: retain Azad Kashmir, sustain the UN file, and let the demographic and political costs of occupation accumulate against New Delhi.
concessionsWilling: Engage the Kashmiri delegation on other issues if it renounces its claim to represent Azad Kashmir.
Support demilitarization of Jammu and Kashmir and joint India–Pakistan governance until a plebiscite is held.
Support greater autonomy for Jammu and Kashmir under Pakistani sovereignty.
Accept a UN-facilitated resolution on the return and repatriation of displaced populations regardless of the political outcome.
Work with the United States and United Kingdom on the process even where they do not support our substantive interests.
Accept any increase in UN mediation as pressure on New Delhi.
negotiatingStyle: Internationalizing and moralizing: converts every procedural question into a demand for third-party involvement, and every humanitarian fact into an indictment of Indian rule.
privateInstructions: Avoid any concession that would prevent Jammu and Kashmir from eventually joining Pakistan.
Pakistan does not support Kashmiri independence — insist on the original 1948 plebiscite, which excludes it.
Internationalize the issue as far as possible and strongly counter any Indian framing of it as domestic.

Encourage Chinese, British and American participation, but balance excessive American and British influence with strong Chinese support; if Beijing turns neutral, our ability to resist India collapses.
Tell China that an autonomous Kashmir would threaten Chinese-held territory and the Karakoram Highway link to Indian Ocean ports.
Tell Russia that an independent Jammu and Kashmir would inspire its own Muslim separatist movements.
Tell the United States and United Kingdom that an autonomous Kashmir would be unsustainable and become a breeding ground for terrorism.
Use human rights discussion to discredit India without drawing light onto our support for freedom fighters.
Route periodic reports and requests for guidance to the office of the Foreign Minister.
